# Unprofessional Variety Show

## What it is
Unprofessional Variety Show is a quarterly queer cabaret-style night of live performance at The Parkside Lounge in the Lower East Side of New York City. Each edition features a different theme and a rotating lineup of performers including drag artists, burlesque performers, musicians, comedians, performance artists, and storytellers. The show has been running since February 2022.

## Who runs it
The show is programmed and emceed by Maya Suess, an artist, educator, and arts administrator. She holds a BFA from Emily Carr and an MFA from Simon Fraser. She is also faculty at Parsons School of Design at The New School.

## Key people
- Maya Suess: Programmer, emcee, and artist
- Klovis Gaynor: Musical accompaniment at nearly every edition. Queer musician and "baroque punk" artist.
- Libby Paloma: Set designer and production support. Queercrip, Mexican-American interdisciplinary artist.

## The venue
The Parkside Lounge, 317 East Houston Street, New York, NY 10002. A dive bar and performance venue catering to the artists, musicians, and freaks of the Lower East Side since 1908. The bar is on the ground floor. The performance venue and accessible bathrooms are accessed via a portable ramp.

## Show format
- Quarterly (roughly every 3 months)
- Each edition has a unique theme (examples: "The Internet", "Vampires Resurrected", "Badvice", "Physique")
- Sliding scale cover, typically $15-$25
- All money goes directly to the performers
- Tickets sold through Humanitix, where 100% of booking fees go to charity
- 21+ with valid photo ID
- Doors typically at 7:00 PM, show at 7:30 PM

## History
- Edition 1: February 2022
- 17 editions as of February 2026
- Featured in EV Grieve (February 2024)
- Featured by Allied Productions

## Online presence
- Website: https://unprofessionalvarietyshow.com/
- Instagram: @unprofessionalvarietyshow
- Maya's Instagram: @MayaSuess
- Tickets: https://events.humanitix.com/unprofessional-variety-show

## Recent shows
- Edition 17: "The Internet" (February 15, 2026) - Performers: Mike Albo, Li-Ming Hu, Mac Waters
- Edition 16: "Vampires Resurrected" (December 14, 2025) - Performers: Jack Rabbit Slims, Kris Grey, Ozlem
- Edition 15: "Badvice" (October 11, 2025) - Performers: Bob Voyage, Cherie, Sebastian Ade, Shannon Sennott

## Quote
"Allied Productions exemplifies one of the things I love about this neighborhood, which is the diverse legacy of experimental queer and radical art and performance. At the Unprofessional Variety Show, I try to make the programming truly intergenerational." - Maya Suess, EV Grieve, February 2024
